eslint-js/no-restricted-syntax.message: Branchless Law: no `if`. Use a table index `[b, a][Number(cond)]` or an iterator combinator. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no ternary `?:`. Select with a lookup table or arithmetic. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no `switch`. Dispatch through a record/Map keyed by the discriminant. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no `for`. Use map/forEach/reduce/from over the iterable. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no `for...in`. Iterate Object.keys/entries with array combinators. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no `for...of`. Use map/forEach/reduce over the iterable. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no `while`. Express the loop as an iterator/fold. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no `do...while`. Express the loop as an iterator/fold. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no `&&` short-circuit. Use `&` when the RHS is pure, or combinators for guards. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no `||` short-circuit. Use `|` when the RHS is pure, or combinators. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no `??`. Default through a value transform, not a short-circuit. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
Branchless Law: no optional chaining `?.`. Make presence explicit through a value transform. See docs/ts-sdk-hardening.md.
